channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Black Ops 7 Season 3 Reloaded Details Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Black Ops 7 Season 3 Reloaded details are out, revealing new content for Endgame, multiplayer, Zombies, and Warzone.

Call of Duty has been doing a lot for Season 3, introducing Dave Chappelle Operator to the game, a brainrot-inspired map for April fools, and much more. Now, it’s time for Season 3 Reloaded to drop on April 30, 2026, bringing a plethora of mid-season content for players to check out.

Black Ops 7 Season 3 Reloaded Overview

Black Ops 7 Season 3 Reloaded is a mid-season update jam-packed with new content. Players will receive new content across all modes, as well as new experiences such as Heat Wave Havoc and Hot Pursuit. Players will also receive more maps, game modes, a fresh round-based Zomvies map, weapons, blueprints, operator skins, events, and challenges.

Endgame Content

Call of Duty: Black Ops 6 endgame will remain free-to-play for a limited time, and players will also see the introduction of Act II: Operation Broken Mirror. This Operation will introduce new challenges, rewards, and the Virus Injection Glitch. There’s also a new major ability called Thermal Spike Major, which can help set enemies on fire. There’s also a new activity called Mega Abomination, which introduces a new boss in Nightmare Zones once you defeat the Guild Strike boss.

Black Ops 7 Season 3 Reloaded Multiplayer Content: Maps and Modes

Coming to multiplayer content in Black Ops 7 Season 3 Reloaded, players should look out for the Onsen hot springs map, which can be used for 6v6 as well as 2v2 matches. Remastered Summit and Hacienda will also be arriving. Freerun is a new game mode that will have you competing against other players across 10 zones of dexterity and marksmanship to post the best time. Other modes include Heat Wave, Havoc, and Freeze Tag.

Black Ops 7 Season 3 Reloaded Zombies Content: New Round-Based Map, Enemies, Wonder Weapon, and New Features

Regarding the Black Ops 7 Season 3 Reloaded Zombies content, players will receive the new Totenreich round-based map. Here you will visit a Norwegian fishing village, and explore the unsettling relationship between the locals and the Group 935 research facility. There’s a new enemy to look out for, the Necropincer, who can attack you from underneath with his trident and mutated claws.

The new Wonder Weapon is called Jotunn Star, a melee weapon that can burn enemies with spectral energy. Players can also flame zombies with the Flammenfalle Trap or use the Wild Fire Field Upgrade for even more firepower.

Warzone Content

Coming to Call of Duty’s battle royale title, it also features a whole bunch of new content for Season 3 Reloaded. Firstly, there are new limited-time modes such as Hot Pursuit, which tests players’ driving skills in Avalon, and Prop Hunt Royale, a 24v24 party mode for Rebirth Island.

Then we have Spikes coming to Buy Stations, which provide operators with improved combat capabilities, including Armor Salvaging, Prone Firing, and Handicraft. Additionally, Cluster Grenades are arriving for Core Battle Royale and Resurgence modes.

Black Ops 7 Season 3 Reloaded Weapons, Attachments and Events

The details everybody has been waiting for, the new weapons for Black Ops 7 Season 3 Reloaded have been revealed, which you can check out below:

Weapons

Special Weapon: Siren (In-Season)

  • Special, Event Reward
  • Levels: 30
  • MAGS: 3
  • MAG SIZE: 6
  • Mastery Badges: Yes
  • Attachments: Magazine, Rear Grip
  • Customize Options: Blueprints, Camo Unlocks (MP, ZM, WZ), Accessories (1), Decals (1), Stickers (3).
  • Prestige Attachment: Magazine > MFS Deflection Core
  • The Universal Weapon Prestige Camos for the Siren (above): Prestige 1: “Racer.” Prestige 2: “Dionaea.” Prestige Max: “It’s Corn.”

Melee Weapon: Katana (In-Season)

  • Melee, Event Reward
  • Levels: 30
  • Mastery Badges: Yes
  • Attachments: None
  • Customize Options: Blueprints
  • The Universal Weapon Prestige Camos for the Katana (above): Prestige 1: “Splash.” Prestige 2: “Sour Raspberry.” Prestige Max: “Enamored.”

Attachments

MK.78 Lightframe PDW Conversion

  • MK.78 LMG, Weekly Challenge Reward
  • Attachment > Stock

Warden 308 Badlands Pistol Kit

  • Warden 308 Marksman Rifle, Weekly Challenge Reward
  • Attachment > Comb

Warden 308 Badlands Pistol Kit

  • Warden 308 Marksman Rifle, Weekly Challenge Reward
  • Attachment > Comb

Carbon 57 Fabricator Mag

  • Carbon 57 SMG, Weekly Challenge Reward
  • Attachment > Magazine

Ryden 45K Apex Sweeper Rig

  • Ryden 45K SMG, Weekly Challenge Reward
  • Attachment > Fire Mod

Events

  • RoboCop Event Pass
  • Freerun Ascent Challenge
  • Call of Duty Endowment C.O.D.E. Navigator Challenge
  • Multiplayer Ranked Series
  • Call of Duty: Warzone Ranked Series

All the details on Black Ops 7 Season 3 Reloaded Content were made available through Activision’s latest blog post.

What are your thoughts on Call of Duty Black Ops 7 Season 3 Reloaded? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Conan Exiles Enhanced receiving an Enhanced Endowment Slider. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced PC System Requirements Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Ubisoft has revealed all the minimum, recommended, high, and ultra system requirements players will need to run Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced on PC.

Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced has finally been officially revealed after Ubisoft failed to keep most of the details under wraps. We already know all the visual and technical improvements in the Black Flag Remake; however, here are the specs you’ll need to run the title on your PC.

Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced PC System Requirements Aren’t Too Demanding

Looking at the system requirements, players can see that Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced won’t take much to run on minimum graphics settings. The lowest entry point starts with NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1660 (6GB), AMD Radeon RX5500XT (8GB), or Intel ARC A580 (8GB) GPUs, paired with Intel Core i7-8700K 3.7 GHz or AMD Ryzen 5 3600 3.6 GHz CPUs.

The highest it goes is NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4090 (24GB) or AMD Radeon RX7900XTX (24GB) GPUs with Intel Core i7-12700K 3.6 GHz, AMD Ryzen 7 5700X3D 3 GHz CPUs, which can provide 4K at 60 FPS. Additionally, a 65 GB SSD and 12GB RAM are required for any of the setups.

Check out the system requirements table below to see if your PC can run Black Flag Resynced:

The Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced PC System Requirements were found on Ubisoft’s website.

In other news, a reworked Animus returns in Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced with new moments. The upcoming title will not feature multiplayer or DLC. What are your thoughts on Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced’s PC system requirements? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced not coming to Switch at launch. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Is Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced On PlayStation, Xbox, PC, Or Nintendo Switch?
item.content:encoded:
Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced has finally been officially unveiled, as gaming’s worst-kept secret has come to an end. Now, it’s time to really get excited, but before you do, you’ll need to know if you can play it!

The original Black Flag came out two console generations ago, but a lot has changed since then, including the new gameplay. Now, with a host of upgrades and improvements, Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced is made for modern devices, but does that include them all?

To find out which platforms Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced will be playable on, you’re in the right place: Insider Gaming has all the details below.

Will Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Be On PlayStation?

PlayStation fans, get ready to sail the seven seas once again, as Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced is arriving on the PlayStation 5 this Summer.

  • More Insider Gaming: Don’t Worry, Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Isn’t An RPG

Assassin’s Creed titles have never missed the PlayStation consoles, and that streak continues with Black Flag Resynced. Just like you could over 10 years ago, you’ll be able to guide Edward Kenway on his pirate’s journey through the Caribbean on the PS5.

Will Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Be On Xbox?

Just as it was way back when, Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced will also launch on Xbox Series X|S.

It should go without saying that Black Flag Resynced won’t be launching on past-gen consoles, like the Xbox One or PS4, as this ground-up remake is exclusive to current-gen consoles instead.

Will Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Be On PC?

As expected, Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced will be fully playable on PC at launch.

  • More Insider Gaming: Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Releases July 9, 2026

In fact, you’ll have a choice as to where you download it from, with the remake being available on both Ubisoft Connect and Steam. Regardless of where you buy it, though, expect the same game through and through—a high-seas and high-stakes adventure.

Will Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Be On Nintendo Switch?

Unfortunately, it’s Nintendo fans who miss out once again, as Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced won’t be launching on either of the Nintendo Switch consoles.

It’s a shame, too, as Black Flag on the go would be elite, but based on the visual overhaul, it might be too comprehensive a title for the hardware to handle. That being said, there’s every chance it could make its way onto the Switch 2 in the future, though that simply remains a hope for now.

Where will you be buying Assassin’s Creed Black Flag resynced? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming platform hubs, check out the following:

  • Invincible VS
  • Forza Horizon 6
  • NTE: Neverness To Everness

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

item.title: Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Not Coming To Nintendo Switch At Launch
item.content:encoded:
The worst-kept secret is out: Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced is coming out in 2026. This is amazing news for long-time AC fans, but it comes with a caveat if you love the Nintendo Switch or Switch 2.

Everything we’ve seen of Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced is incredible. Ubisoft has done a great job making a 13-year-old game look brand new. It’s coming to all major platforms and consoles, except for either of Nintendo‘s main hardware, sadly.

Nintendo Switch Missing Out On Black Flag Resynced Pirate Party

It’s time for a sad sea shanty, as Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced will not be releasing on the Nintendo Switch or Switch 2 on July 9, 2026.

The Ubisoft store listing for the game clearly states the platforms, and none of the Switch devices are included.

It’s unclear if this is a hardware issue and the Switch 2 not being able to run the game, which would be odd as AC Shadows runs and plays on the Switch 2, or if it’s something else.

This is speculation on my part, but a future Switch 2 port shouldn’t be ruled out.

Practically all Assassin’s Creed games have eventually launched on the Switch and Switch 2, so don’t go keelhauling yourself over this disappointment just yet!

Which version of Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced will you be buying? Do you think you’ll stump up some extra gold for the alluring Collector’s Edition? Let us know via the Insider Gaming Discord.

Also, for more on Resynced, make sure you read other popular stories:

  • Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Features Reworked Animus With New Moments
  • Resynced Showcase Confirms Lack of DLC And Multiplayer
  • Edward Kenway’s Voice Actor Returns For Black Flag Resynced
  • Don’t Worry, Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Isn’t An RPG
  • Revamped Tailing Missions Shown During AC Black Flag Resynced Showcase
  • Black Flag Resynced Combat ‘Reworked To An Action-Oriented Experience’

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: Invincible VS Story Details & Character Pass Revealed
item.content:encoded:
New story details have been revealed for Invincible VS, showcasing an original story unique to the game, from the same team behind the show.

As if that wasn’t exciting enough, the first two characters for the Year 1 Character Pass have also been revealed, as Universa and The Immortal will be the first to join the base roster.

Invincible VS Offers A Unique Story

Until now, news regarding the game’s story mode had been scarce. Focus had been on the gameplay itself, which features 3v3 tag fighting and incredibly gory combat.

Well, the first trailer for the campaign has now been shared online, and it teases an entirely unique story separate from the show and comics. It’s clear that it will revolve around the Viltrumites invading Earth, with the heroes from across the planet desperate to stop them.

At the centre of the conflict is Omni Man, who appears dead set on conquering Earth with his Viltrumite companions—a clear departure from the show’s narrative. Expect Conquest and Anissa to face off against Bulletproof and Rex Splode, as uneven as those matchups might sound!

Once you’re done with the campaign and have mastered the base roster of 18 characters, you can look forward to new arrivals with the Character Pass. Arriving this Summer will be both Universa and The Immortal, with two more set to arrive before the year closes: one in the Fall, and the other in Winter.

Considering that it explicitly mentions Year One Character Pass, it should be safe to assume that more Passes will arrive in the future. With a character line-up as vast as Invincible’s, it’s fair to say they could add new fighters for years to come. Check out the trailer for yourself down below.

Are you going to be playing the Invincible VS campaign or largely focusing on multiplayer battles? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, read about Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced not being an RPG, as well as everything announced during the Arpil 2026 ID@Xbox Showcase.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…
